stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 5 observations with mean 48.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.8). region B has 8 observations with mean 38.3 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.4). region C has 20 observations with mean 45.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 7.4). region D has 8 observations with mean 47.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.5). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.7 and 11.7 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region C: 45.6 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 45.6). Range [45.5, 45.6] as between-region SD varies from 4.7 to 11.7. Pooled mean: 45.0.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 45.0 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=7.8: B = 0.957.
Interval [45.5, 45.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (45.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (45.0) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
